projectDescription: A teaching-oriented deep-dive into AI agent architecture, featuring a minimal Python agent implementation and comprehensive research documentation on agent runtime design patterns.
keyFeatures: Minimal Python agent implementation with pluggable LLM interface
Comprehensive deep-dive research documentation on AI agent design
Tool registration and execution system with permission controls
Skills discovery mechanism via SKILL.md convention
Streaming LLM output with FakeLLM for local development
Multi-turn agent runtime loop with max_turns safeguard
Message model with role-based conversation history
Agent orchestration: main agent, verification agent, subagents
Memory system for session context injection
publicApiSurface: Agent.run(user_input: str) -> str — execute agent main loop
Agent.register_tool(tool: Tool) -> None — register a callable tool
Agent.load_skills(skills_dir: str | Path) -> list[str] — discover skills
Agent.remember(text: str) -> None — add to memory
Tool.call(payload: dict) -> ToolResult — invoke a tool
ToolResult(ok: bool, content: str, meta: dict) — tool output
Message(role: str, content: str, meta: dict) — conversation message
echo_tool(payload: dict) -> ToolResult — built-in echo tool
